title: PR #61 was canon-conformant in the letter but shipped three brittleness vectors that the conformance review (mine) did not catch
content: Pre-merge canon review of PR #61 (commit 152f2ee, branch claude/portal-bootstrap-ride-along-0dECk) verified: D0025 portal route ownership intact, D0026 two-worker topology intact (both wrangler.toml files structural), D0027 ride-along delivery in worker/src/mcp.ts canon-faithful (withRideAlong matches §Mechanism conceptual sketch nearly verbatim, RIDE_ALONG_BUDGET=64 bounded, ams_recv correctly un-wrapped, two-way-door reversibility intact, no new state, no domain logic). The bootstrap-render side passed every constraint-checklist item: render-time fetching, content negotiation across text/html / text/markdown / application/json / */*, frozen-fallback gated on canon-unreachable AND no cache. Tooled and constraint-grounded review approved the PR for merge. Operator's vibe-level 'this is brittle' reaction caught what conformance review missed: (a) handrolled markdown blockquote parser (~40 lines walking ## heading matches and >-prefixed bodies, silently fails on canon shape drift), (b) six exact-string section heading matches against canon prose with U+2014 em-dashes (any rename → silent fallback), (c) frozenFallback() with hardcoded duplicate copy of all six prescribed sections — exactly the anti-pattern portal-bootstrap-content §The Living-Canon Posture forbids: 'Do not freeze the prescribed text in source.' Conformance to the letter of the constraint coexisted with violation of its spirit because the constraint's 'fetch mechanism is implementation choice' clause invited the lowest-effort path.

title: PR #61 already merged at 03:47 UTC before the brittleness conversation; cursor-agent landed a $-escape fix on the branch first
content: Timeline: 03:24 UTC end of canon review turn → 03:30 UTC cursor-agent commit 31f06e0 'fix(tincan): escape dollar-sign patterns in bootstrap replacements' (function-replacement form to defeat $$, $&, $`, $' interpretation in String.prototype.replace) pushed to PR branch → 03:47 UTC PR #61 merged to main as commit dc0610d → 03:48 UTC PR branch deleted → 12:23 UTC operator 'wait, brittle!' message starts the rewrite turn. The rewrite that the operator was asking for had to land as a new PR (#64) against current main, not a revision of the closed PR. First push attempt re-created the deleted branch as an orphan because local repo state was stale; orphan was deleted after rebasing onto current main.

title: Handroll-vs-borrow has the same shape on canon ingest as it does on MCP — oddkit IS the resolution layer for canon, just like @modelcontextprotocol/sdk IS for MCP
content: The resolution layer already exists (oddkit for canon, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk for MCP), and writing a parser on top is the failure mode that ams://canon/constraints/borrow-evaluation-before-implementation was written to prevent. PR #61 shipped a handrolled markdown blockquote parser plus frozen prose for canon ingest, even though portal-bootstrap-content §Render-Time Composition explicitly named oddkit MCP client as one of three implementation options. Tooled canon-conformance review approved it because the implementation conformed to the letter of the constraint. The pattern: any time a project has a maintained resolution layer for its own knowledge surface, agents will reach past it to handroll unless the constraint AND the review both close that path. Same lesson as ams://canon/constraints/mcp-build-side-governance, transposed from MCP to canon ingest. Promotion candidate for klappy.dev: extend mcp-build-side-governance into a broader 'use the maintained resolution layer for your own knowledge surface' constraint, OR write a sibling AMS constraint specifically for canon-ingest in oddkit-powered projects. Either form would close the path the operator had to close manually this round.

title: Conformance review and craft review are different reviews — operator-as-validator caught the brittleness because tooled review can only check what canon explicitly says
content: Canon-conformance review (does the PR satisfy each item in the constraint?) is mechanical and tractable for an LLM. Craft review (is this code maintainable? would a human want to debug it in six months?) requires judgment calibrated against operational pain that canon does not enumerate. portal-bootstrap-content §The Living-Canon Posture says 'Do not freeze the prescribed text in source' but does not say 'Do not write a markdown parser by hand' — that judgment lives in adjacent canon (mcp-build-side-governance, borrow-evaluation-before-implementation) and the agent has to make the cross-link. The agent did not make the cross-link in PR #61 nor in the initial review of it. The operator did, in one sentence. This is the operator-as-validator pattern from the Linux/layer-separation and TinCan-as-brand catches in the standing memory: tooled validation reliably surfaces missing prerequisites within the constraint at hand but cannot reliably evaluate cross-canon coherence or maintenance posture. The procedural fix is to add an explicit cross-canon check to canon-conformance reviews — at minimum a 'does this borrow from any maintained resolution layer the project already runs?' question.

title: Bootstrap.ts uses oddkit MCP client (six parallel oddkit_get tools/calls) for canon ingest; no handrolled markdown parser; no frozen prose
content: PR #64 lands on top of merged PR #61. Replaces the handrolled extractBlockquotedSection + six exact-string heading matches + frozenFallback() with: six parallel oddkit_get JSON-RPC tools/calls to https://oddkit.klappy.dev/mcp, each addressing CONSTRAINT_URI ams://canon/constraints/portal-bootstrap-content + section name (oddkit owns section addressing); one peelBlockquote() helper (~10 lines, no ## heading matching) for canon's universal contiguous-> shape; in-isolate per-section cache for the constraint's recommended 24h freshness budget; stale cache served on transient blip, no cache + no canon = throw → route handler returns 503 bootstrap_unavailable with the underlying reason in the message and a console.error for Workers Logs. Cursor-agent's 31f06e0 $-escape fix preserved (function-replacement form). Public surface unchanged (renderBootstrapMarkdown, renderBootstrapJson, negotiateAccept, BootstrapInputs, ConvRecord); portal.ts unchanged; mcp.ts unchanged. Verified end-to-end against live oddkit before commit: 6 sections fetched in 1.5s cold / 700ms warm, nested blockquote in 'Required Before Joining' preserved, all five template substitutions clean, no leaked template variables, tsc --strict --noUncheckedIndexedAccess passes.

title: When canon supplies a maintained resolution layer (oddkit for klappy ecosystem; equivalently the maintained MCP SDK for /mcp surfaces), implementation borrows it; handrolling against the underlying transport requires a named justification
content: Operative form for AMS: any code that ingests canon (URIs in the ams:// or klappy:// schemes) goes through oddkit_get / oddkit_search via the oddkit MCP client. Direct fetches against raw.githubusercontent.com or the GitHub API for canon paths are a smell — they bypass section addressing, content-hash provenance, and the oddkit governance layer (telemetry, supersession-walking, freshness budget). Justification required for any non-oddkit canon ingest path. Mirrors mcp-build-side-governance for the MCP wrapper surface; this constraint generalizes the same discipline to canon ingest. Until promoted to canon (operator gate), this is a journaled-only operative constraint.

content: Open items for the next session or operator action: (1) PR #64 — bootstrap-via-oddkit-mcp rewrite — open against main (head 0433c10), 2 files, +250/-185, awaiting operator merge per project PR discipline. title: Initial rewrite push reproduced the original $-replace bug — copy-pasting the prior implementation's String.replace shape carried the bug forward despite knowing about cursor-agent's fix
content: First push of the rewrite (orphan branch claude/portal-bootstrap-ride-along-0dECk, commit 23a8334) used the bare-string form .replace(/{namespace}/g, value) — same shape as PR #61 pre-31f06e0. Caught only by checking the actual main HEAD before opening the new PR and seeing the cursor-agent fix. Mitigation in PR #64 (commit 0433c10): function-replacement form .replace(/{x}/g, () => value), which avoids backreference interpretation entirely and is more bug-resistant than the $-escape form. Open question for procedural canon: rewrites that delete-and-replace files lose downstream fixes by default; the fix-discovery step (compare new file against current HEAD) should be ritual when the file being replaced has accumulated post-PR fixes. Worth journaling because the same failure mode is likely to recur on the mcp.ts SDK rewrite (P0002 step 4), which is a much bigger delete-and-replace.
